_note: laptop-scale validation (14-core contended Mac; injector cores start at 2, Envoy disjoint 8-13/conc6; SHORT 4-stage stair topping at 8000, C in {1,2} only). PROVES THE LOGIC: per-C cpuset pinning works, the clean-gate + reuse assertion run per C, Envoy stays unsaturated (14% / 9% — well under its 6-core budget), and rps_per_core is computed and FALLS as C grows. Absolute numbers are NOT meaningful here — the short stair + a contended laptop make the per-C ceilings nearly identical (both ~2000, capped by the stair top, not by cores), so the apparent rps/core drop is dominated by clean-gate point selection, not a true curve. The real diminishing-returns curve (C=1 ~5k, C=2 ~9k, C=4 ~15k, C=8 ~20k with rps/core falling) needs the CI box: idle 16 vCPU, full 20k stair, C in {1,2,4,8}. Replace with a fresh CI run.
